master_row.title: Quantum Phase Transitions
master_row.canonical_statement: A quantum phase transition occurs when the ground state of a system on the Trawinistic manifold changes non-analytically as a function of a control parameter at zero temperature; the critical point is characterized by divergent correlation length determined by the Trawinistic Laplacian eigenvalues.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating ξ, ν, g, g_c.
master_row.dictionary: A quantum phase transition occurs when the ground state of a system on the Trawinistic manifold changes non‑analytically as a function of a control parameter at zero temperature; the critical point is characterized by divergent correlation length determined by the Trawinistic Laplacian eigenvalues.
master_row.encyclopedia: Quantum criticality is marked by gap closure and divergence of correlation length. Interpretive role: This entry gives the registry a way to talk about genuinely new ground-state structure rather than small perturbative drift. It also ties spectral data, confinement, and renormalization language together in a single criticality framework.
